name: stripe
context: Stripe payments platform — charges, subscriptions, checkout, webhooks, and Connect marketplace
info: The Stripe ecosystem covers online payment processing, recurring billing, hosted checkout pages, webhook event handling, and multi-party marketplace payments via Connect. Stripe provides a unified API for accepting credit cards, bank transfers, and dozens of local payment methods worldwide. The platform handles PCI compliance, fraud detection, and regulatory requirements. This tree is organized around the jobs an agent actually performs: creating payment intents, setting up subscriptions, building checkout flows, handling webhook events, and managing Connect accounts. The stripe-node SDK is the primary server-side library. All operations support test mode with `sk_test_` keys.
body:
# Stripe Ecosystem

Stripe is a payments platform where agents create payment intents, manage subscriptions, build checkout flows, handle webhook events, and orchestrate marketplace payments. The Stripe API provides a unified interface for accepting payments across cards, bank transfers, wallets, and local payment methods in 195+ countries.

## Agent Jobs

The primary tasks an agent performs in this ecosystem:

- **Create payment intents** — build server-side payment flows, confirm charges, and handle 3D Secure authentication. See [[stripe/payments]].
- **Set up subscriptions** — create recurring billing with prices, trial periods, and automatic invoicing. See [[stripe/subscriptions]].
- **Build checkout flows** — generate hosted checkout sessions for one-time and recurring payments. See [[stripe/checkout]].
- **Handle webhooks** — receive and verify Stripe events for payment confirmations, subscription changes, and disputes. See [[stripe/webhooks]].
- **Manage Connect accounts** — onboard sellers, split payments, and handle payouts in marketplace scenarios. See [[stripe/connect]].

## API Overview

All Stripe API requests use `https://api.stripe.com` as the base URL. Authentication is via Bearer token using your secret key. The API is RESTful with consistent resource patterns:

| Operation | Pattern | Example |
|-----------|---------|----------|
| Create | `POST /v1/{resource}` | `POST /v1/payment_intents` |
| Retrieve | `GET /v1/{resource}/{id}` | `GET /v1/payment_intents/pi_xxx` |
| Update | `POST /v1/{resource}/{id}` | `POST /v1/payment_intents/pi_xxx` |
| List | `GET /v1/{resource}` | `GET /v1/payment_intents?limit=10` |
| Delete | `DELETE /v1/{resource}/{id}` | `DELETE /v1/customers/cus_xxx` |

## Authentication

Stripe uses API keys for authentication:

- **Secret key** (`sk_test_...` / `sk_live_...`) — server-side only, full API access
- **Publishable key** (`pk_test_...` / `pk_live_...`) — client-side, limited to tokenization
- **Restricted keys** — scoped permissions for specific resources

```javascript
import Stripe from 'stripe';
const stripe = new Stripe(process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY);
```

## Test Mode

All skills in this tree work with test mode keys (`sk_test_`). Use Stripe's test card numbers:

| Card | Number | Behavior |
|------|--------|----------|
| Visa (success) | `4242424242424242` | Always succeeds |
| Visa (decline) | `4000000000000002` | Always declines |
| 3D Secure | `4000002500003155` | Requires authentication |
| Insufficient funds | `4000000000009995` | Insufficient funds decline |

## Libraries

- **stripe-node** — official Node.js/TypeScript SDK
- **stripe-python** — official Python SDK
- **stripe-ruby** — official Ruby SDK
- **stripe-go** — official Go SDK
- **Stripe CLI** — local webhook testing, event forwarding, log tailing

sections.title: API overview
sections.body: The Stripe API at `https://api.stripe.com` follows RESTful conventions. All resources support create, retrieve, update, list, and (where applicable) delete. Responses use JSON. Pagination uses cursor-based `starting_after` and `ending_before` parameters. Idempotency keys prevent duplicate operations. See [[stripe/payments]] for payment-specific endpoints and [[stripe/subscriptions]] for billing endpoints.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.body: Use secret keys (`sk_test_` / `sk_live_`) for server-side API calls. Publishable keys (`pk_test_` / `pk_live_`) are safe for client-side tokenization. Restricted keys allow scoped permissions per resource. Pass the key via `Authorization: Bearer sk_test_...` header or initialize the SDK with `new Stripe(secretKey)`. Never expose secret keys in client-side code or version control.

sections.title: Test mode
sections.body: Test mode uses `sk_test_` keys and does not process real charges. Use test card numbers like `4242424242424242` for successful payments and `4000000000000002` for declines. The Stripe CLI (`stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/webhook`) forwards test webhook events locally. All [[stripe/payments]], [[stripe/subscriptions]], and [[stripe/checkout]] operations work identically in test mode.
